label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking how the author introduces the topic. The options are A through D. Looking at the beginning of the text, the author starts with a series of questions: "Do you start your work by making a list... Do you always take a list... Do you write down..." So they're asking if the reader does certain things related to making lists. Then they lead into discussing the benefits of being a list maker. The options are: A) asking a series of questions, B) presenting some simple facts, C) stating his own point of view, D) listing the benefits of a to-do list. The first part of the text is definitely a series of questions to engage the reader. Then after that, the author lists the reasons (the benefits) for why making lists is good. But the question is specifically about how the author introduces the topic. The introduction part is the initial questions. The rest of the text is explaining the benefits, but the introduction method is through the questions. So the correct answer
